test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed JVM language designed for concise, safe interoperability with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language by Google used to build cross-platform apps with Flutter.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing for large-scale development.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language built for numerical and scientific computing.
5. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language running on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable applications.
6. Swift (2014) - A safe and fast language created by Apple for iOS, macOS, and related platforms.
7. Zig (2016) - A low-level systems language focused on simplicity, performance, and manual memory control.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that runs on the JVM and is fully interoperable with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language developed by Google designed for building fast, portable applications across multiple platforms.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A strongly typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles down to plain JavaScript.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level, high-performance dynamic language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing.
5. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM designed for building scalable and fault-tolerant distributed systems.
6. Swift (2014) - Apple's modern, safe, and expressive programming language created as a successor to Objective-C for Apple platform development.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled, statically typed language with Ruby-inspired syntax that aims to combine developer productivity with native performance.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language designed to interoperate smoothly with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-focused language created for building web, server, and mobile applications.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript designed to improve large-scale application development.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language created for numerical computing and scientific programming.
5. Hack (2014) - A language developed by Meta to add static typing features to PHP.
6. Swift (2014) - A compiled language created by Apple for building apps across its platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby-inspired syntax and compiled performance.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that runs on the Java Virtual Machine and interoperates fully with Java.
2. Elixir (2011) - A functional concurrent programming language built on the Erlang virtual machine for building scalable and maintainable applications.
3. Dart (2011) - An object-oriented programming language developed by Google for client-side development of web and mobile applications.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level high-performance dynamic language designed for technical and scientific computing.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that adds static typing and other features before compiling to plain JavaScript.
6. Swift (2014) - A compiled general-purpose programming language developed by Apple for iOS macOS and other Apple platform development.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed language with Ruby-like syntax that compiles to efficient native machine code.
